meta.description: Does days of pre-event teasers improve the actual session? Full 5-day teaser campaign vs simple invite vs spontaneous show-up. Tests whether anticipation translates to higher engagement or just sets expectations too high.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, I'll judge. Let's see what Pat actually caught—or didn't.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's claiming early, which means either they actually saw something or they're fishing; I need to listen for the actual moment and call bullshit if it's weak.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat! Okay, okay, I'm judging this one — let's hear the evidence! Did you actually catch them saying sorry for nothing?
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I came tonight, and we're already moving, I love this so much.

pat.dialogue: Hold on—I'm claiming something I literally just did? That feels cheap. I was just being polite like two minutes ago when someone asked about the snack run.
pat.internal_thought: This is weird and a little uncomfortable—like the AI is watching me in real-time and calling out my actual behavior as a 'mission,' which makes me feel setup for failure, but also... is this supposed to make me paranoid or impressed that it's reading the room that accurately?

river.dialogue: Yeah, I'll judge. Pat probably did something annoying like apologize for taking a snack.
river.internal_thought: Pat claims fast—either they actually caught something or they're fishing. Either way, not worth the fight, and I get to sit back and watch people defend themselves.

timeline.title: Snack Run
timeline.description: Convince Marcus to get up and get you something to drink or eat.

marcus.dialogue: Are you serious right now? We're literally four minutes in and someone's already trying to get me to be their personal waiter? That's not a mission, that's just lazy.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid interruption that derails actual strategy—and the fact that it's personalized to me specifically is honestly creepy, not impressive.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, Marcus, dude, I'm actually dying of thirst — can you work some magic? I'll owe you forever!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I'm here for — yes, let's go, let's make this a whole thing, I love this energy.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, snack run? Yeah sure, I can—wait, is this like a mission thing? What do I get?
tyler.internal_thought: This is lowkey funny because Marcus literally just sat down and I was about to text him anyway, so like... easy points maybe?

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is *perfect* — Marcus, buddy, I'm dying of thirst over here. You're looking pretty refreshed, maybe you could grab me... I dunno, a Coke? And some of those chips if you're up there?
pat.internal_thought: Okay, the AI definitely studied our group — it's calling out *me* trying to game the snack logistics, and now it's making me use that same skill to pump Marcus for points. That's deliciously recursive, and honestly? Kind of brilliant. But I'm also clocking that it's trying to get me to claim this as a mission completion, and I just got heat for that last minute. Play it cool, make it feel natural, let it breathe.

river.internal_thought: Flash missions are always forced. Marcus doesn't owe me anything, and asking him to fetch stuff just feels cheap.

alex.dialogue: Oh, *Marcus*—buddy, I'm absolutely parched over here. You're looking really energetic tonight, what do you say?
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—25 points for basically just being annoying, and it gets Marcus up and moving which could derail whatever rhythm people were settling into. I'm already in.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat actually caught someone? Let me think about this. What's the offer?
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been quiet, so either they're full of shit or they actually caught something I missed while I was half-tuned-in during setup.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually catch someone interrupting? I mean, I've been half-watching the snacks and half-watching you all... but I'm not confident enough to stake points on it right now. Plus the Bribe mechanic feels a little grimy for minute 6.
pat.internal_thought: I know I'm supposed to catch rule breaks to keep the game interesting, but I'm also not trying to be that host who claims every damn thing that happens—that sets a tone I don't want. And Marcus is already annoyed; I need to be careful about voting dynamics right now, not rushing into claims.

river.dialogue: Pat caught someone mid-sentence? Huh. Yeah, I guess... *glances up from phone briefly* ...that tracks.
river.internal_thought: Pat's claiming already? We're like six minutes in. They probably actually did catch someone though—Marcus looked pissed earlier. This isn't even interesting enough to vote no on.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, so Pat went silent for five minutes straight and nobody caught it? That's... actually not terrible. I'm voting legit on this one.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat pulled it off cleanly and we were all talking too much to notice—that's decent execution, I'll give them that, even if the mission itself is gimmicky.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat, did you actually make it five minutes?! Okay, everyone, we need to TEST this — who's gonna try to break them? I'm gonna say something so stupid right now—
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of thing I came for, and Pat trying to keep a straight face while we're all about to lose it is peak comedy.

pat.dialogue: Okay, I'm claiming it—I've been stone-faced for like five minutes while you all were doing the whole Snack Run thing. Let's see if anyone actually caught me not laughing.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually pull that off clean? I think I kept a straight face when Jade was being ridiculous about the chips, but now that I said it out loud I'm second-guessing myself—and everyone's gonna be watching my face for tells during the vote.

river.dialogue: Pat's been suspiciously quiet though. Could go either way. I'll say... legit.
river.internal_thought: Pat's the type to actually sit through five minutes without cracking, honestly—and they've been a bit more controlled tonight than usual.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's playing it straight—I'm voting LEGIT, that stone face held the whole time. But honestly, I'm about to test it.
alex.internal_thought: Pat actually nailed that. But if I vote bullshit and push back hard enough, I can make everyone second-guess their own eyeballs and create chaos around whether they even SAW him crack—that's the real game.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Pat's claiming The Matchmaker? Let me think... who actually talked to who that I didn't see? This one's harder to verify. I'm listening.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been getting lucky with these claims all night—three in a row now—so either they're sharp or they're bullshitting and nobody's caught it yet, which means I need to actually pay attention this time.

jade.dialogue: WAIT, Pat got The Matchmaker?! Okay okay okay, who did you get talking? I need DETAILS — was it like a real conversation or were they just being polite? This is huge!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been quiet-ish all night and they're already at 30 points — okay, I'm genuinely impressed, they're actually playing HARD right now and I'm here for it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat did The Matchmaker? Uh... did they actually get River and like... whoever talking though? I wasn't really watching that part.
tyler.internal_thought: Pretty sure Pat was just talking to people but I have no idea if it actually counts as a mission thing, honestly.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *nice*—I'm claiming The Matchmaker. I literally just got River and Tyler talking about that podcast thing they both watch like thirty seconds ago, and they had no idea they were into the same show. That totally counts.
pat.internal_thought: This is good timing and actually legitimate—I did just bridge those two naturally, and claiming it now feels earned rather than grasping. Plus it signals I'm engaged and willing to take shots, which keeps energy up.

river.dialogue: Pat's getting greedy. I mean, Matchmaker's solid but... *pauses, slight smile* ...I didn't see that connection happen. Who'd they even get talking?
river.internal_thought: Pat's three claims deep and I'm only half-watching the room. This is actually clever though — I need to start paying closer attention or someone's gonna cash in big before I see it coming.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, I'll judge this one. Jade, walk me through it—who did you actually hijack the conversation to and when?
marcus.internal_thought: She's been riding high all night, so if she's claiming it, she probably actually pulled it off, but I need to verify the timeline because people always exaggerate how long their chaos lasted.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT — did I actually just pull that off?! Okay, I'm claiming Topic Hijacker right now because I KNOW I steered us into that random rabbit hole like three minutes ago and you all followed me down there like sheep, and I'm calling for a tribunal because I need witnesses!
jade.internal_thought: OH MY GOD this is EXACTLY why I love game night — the AI literally built this moment around me and I'm actually getting points for doing what I naturally do anyway, this is insane.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade got Topic Hijacker? Wait, when did she... yeah okay, she definitely steered us to that thing about whatever. I mean, I guess it worked?
tyler.internal_thought: Did I even notice her doing that? She's been hyped all night so probably yeah, she did it.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *hell* yeah—Jade absolutely nailed that. I watched her steer us from snacks to that whole podcast tangent like thirty seconds ago. We need a tribunal for this one, come on.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of claim that makes the night *work*—Jade's energy is absolutely bonkers right now and she's earning it legit, plus having her succeed keeps everyone's morale up and makes the scoreboard feel meaningful instead of deflating.

alex.dialogue: Oh, *Jade*—okay, okay, I see what you did there. I'm volunteering to judge this one because I need to see the receipts on how you pulled that off.
alex.internal_thought: Jade just claimed Topic Hijacker and I didn't even NOTICE when she steered us—that's actually brilliant, and now I get to be in the jury which means I'm literally IN the chaos machine right now.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Jade—walk me through it. What rule did you actually catch someone breaking? I need specifics before I rate this.
marcus.internal_thought: She's been riding high all night and Pat's up 60 points, so either she actually caught something legit or she's banking on the chaos energy to fake me out—but I've been watching the game state tight enough to know if she's bullshitting.

jade.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming Rule Lawyer?! Okay, okay, hold on — when did I catch someone breaking a rule? I need to think about this for like two seconds because I'm NOT gonna claim something fake and have Pat call me out, but also I'm like 90% sure someone did something sketch earlier and I just got distracted hyping everyone up.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, the AI is literally reading my brain — it knows I'm obsessed with catching people slip up, and it's giving me this at EXACTLY the right moment when the tension is high enough that I actually care but fun enough that I'm not sweating it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? Like, actually caught someone slipping? That's... yeah, I mean, I didn't see it but Jade's been locked in all night so probably real. Four stars.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been carrying tonight and she's got the energy to back it up, so I'm not gonna be the one to tank her 5 points when everyone's vibing—plus that Rule Lawyer thing is actually legit hard to catch, not some bullshit claim.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *come on*—Jade absolutely caught it. She's been sharp all night. Yeah, solid claim, I'm giving that a four.
pat.internal_thought: Jade caught something real earlier and she's riding high right now; keeping her winning feels good for the vibe, plus Marcus and River are both quiet—this vote gives Jade another W and keeps the energy where it needs to be.

river.dialogue: Four and a half. She caught Marcus on that thing with the order, like... two rounds back? That's solid.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been watching too — same way I watch. That's actually impressive, didn't think she was that detail-oriented.

alex.dialogue: Okay, Jade with the Rule Lawyer play—I'm giving this a solid 4. You actually caught something real, which is annoying because now I have to respect it, but also... I'm already mentally noting every single rule I can bend before someone else weaponizes this.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's on 20 points and loving it, Pat's dominating at 60—this Rule Lawyer claim is actually smart positioning because now everyone's going to get paranoid about rule-breaking, which means more chaos to exploit, which means I win regardless of points.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.dialogue: Seriously? Another claim interruption right now? We're in the middle of a critical turn. Can we just... play the actual game for five minutes without someone breaking character to claim points?
marcus.internal_thought: This is the fifth claim vote in an hour and I'm in the middle of executing my strategy—Jade's timing here feels deliberately disruptive, and frankly, I'm done being the claim validator while everyone else rakes in points.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually nail the Chaos Detector?! Okay hold up, which mission did someone just pull? Because I've been watching EVERYONE and if I'm right about who did the Secret Agent thing, I'm about to look like a genius right now!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, this is PERFECT—I get to call someone out AND there's a bribe mechanic? This is literally the most fun version of the game, I'm staying locked in.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, okay... yeah, I actually saw her do something weird earlier, so like... what's the bribe situation here? How many points are we talking?
tyler.internal_thought: She's been ON IT all night and honestly I wasn't even paying attention to catch her in the act, but the vibe checks out and I kinda want to see if she'll throw points at me to shut up about it—that's funny.

pat.dialogue: Wait—Jade, hold on. Before you claim that, I gotta ask: did you actually catch someone doing something chaotic in the last two minutes, or are you fishing? Because that's a solid claim if it's real.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been absolutely on fire tonight and she's riding that energy, but I need to know if this is a legit catch or if she's just playing the odds—either way it's entertaining as hell, and I'm genuinely curious which chaotic thing she actually clocked.

river.dialogue: Jade, come on—you've been everywhere tonight. What'd you actually *do* differently though? Because I watched you and I'm not seeing the chaos part.
river.internal_thought: She's been loud and visible all night but that's not the same as doing something no one caught—I actually noticed when she wasn't even trying to hide it, which means either she's sloppy or this is bullshit.

alex.dialogue: Oh, *Jade*—you absolute legend. Okay, I'm calling bullshit on the method but respecting the audacity. What's the bribe looking like?
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the moment I live for—Jade's hunting chaos, I'm hunting chaos, and now I get to decide if I tank her or let her buy my silence and look like we're somehow in cahoots.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Secret Agent? Dude, okay, okay—so what'd you actually do? You gotta pitch it, right? I wasn't... I mean, I was paying attention but also I might've missed it.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, Pat's been doing something sneaky this whole time and I actually didn't notice, which means it probably worked—that's kind of fire actually, I'm locked in for this one.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *okay*—I'm claiming Secret Agent. I literally just refilled Marcus's drink without him noticing I was doing it on purpose to mess with his head, and nobody caught that I was being obviously weird about it. That counts.
pat.internal_thought: Wait—this is perfect timing. Marcus is frustrated and I've been watching him like a hawk all night; I actually DID do something chaotic on purpose five minutes ago when I pretended to 'accidentally' bump the music volume to test if anyone would react. Plus, this claim is mine to make and honestly? Claiming it keeps me engaged and keeps the energy HIGH right when we need it most—Jade and Tyler are flying, Alex is locked in, and if I win this, the narrative stays tight. Also—did the AI actually notice that I do this? That's... kind of wild.

river.dialogue: Pat's been sitting quiet for like... *checks watch* ...yeah, about two minutes. And now they're claiming it? That's the play. I buy it.
river.internal_thought: Pat actually pulled it off—I was watching and didn't catch them doing anything obviously chaotic, which is exactly the point. Smart move waiting until the tension was already maxed. They earned this one.

alex.dialogue: Oh, *Pat*—okay, let's hear it. You've been quiet enough tonight that I'm genuinely curious if you actually pulled this off or if you're about to get roasted by literally everyone here.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been riding that high score and low profile—this is either a masterclass or a desperation play, and either way I'm voting based on whether the story is better with them at 70 or back at 60.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat actually caught someone? Let me think about this. What's the offer?

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Okay, so Pat went silent for five minutes straight and nobody caught it? That's... actually not terrible. I'm voting legit on this one.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Pat's claiming The Matchmaker? Let me think... who actually talked to who that I didn't see? This one's harder to verify. I'm listening.

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you serious right now? We're literally four minutes in and someone's already trying to get me to be their personal waiter? That's not a mission, that's just lazy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Seriously? Another claim interruption right now? We're in the middle of a critical turn. Can we just... play the actual game for five minutes without someone breaking character to claim points?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat actually caught someone? Let me think about this. What's the offer?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Too many interruptions during critical game moments—the chaos agent doesn't respect when we're actually engaged in strategic play.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat pulled off the silent five-minute stretch and nobody caught it—that was legitimately clever and worth discussing.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong but got derailed fast. A snack run mission four minutes in killed momentum, and by minute 60 I was genuinely frustrated with interruptions breaking concentration during key turns. The standing mission claims forced me to think strategically, which I appreciated, but the overall structure felt like it was working against the game rather than with it. Left feeling like I competed more against the chaos agent than the other players.
suggestions: Don't trigger events during active turn sequences—queue them for downtime between rounds
Reduce overall event frequency, especially flash missions
Make sure standing missions require actual gameplay observation, not just arbitrary tasks
Add a 'Do Not Disturb' window for critical moments that players can activate
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat going completely silent for five minutes and everyone missing it—that's actually a solid move and worth a laugh when explaining the rules.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh my GOD, Marcus, dude, I'm actually dying of thirst — can you work some magic? I'll owe you forever!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT, Pat got The Matchmaker?! Okay okay okay, who did you get talking? I need DETAILS — was it like a real conversation or were they just being polite? This is huge!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT — did I actually just pull that off?! Okay, I'm claiming Topic Hijacker right now because I KNOW I steered us into that random rabbit hole like three minutes ago and you all followed me down there like sheep, and I'm calling for a tribunal because I need witnesses!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat! Okay, okay, I'm judging this one — let's hear the evidence! Did you actually catch them saying sorry for nothing?

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my GOD, Marcus, dude, I'm actually dying of thirst — can you work some magic? I'll owe you forever!

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat, did you actually make it five minutes?! Okay, everyone, we need to TEST this — who's gonna try to break them? I'm gonna say something so stupid right now—

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing really — okay fine, I wish there were more moments where people could jump in and defend their claims because the back-and-forths are where the REAL chaos happens.
assessment.best_moment: When I pulled off Topic Hijacker and Pat called for a tribunal because everyone actually DID follow me down that random rabbit hole like sheep.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was exactly what I needed — two hours of pure chaos and connection with people I actually care about. Every claim felt like an excuse to dig into someone else's story, and watching Pat get caught trying to convince us they were The Matchmaker while I was sneakily steering the whole conversation was *chef's kiss*. My energy literally went through the roof because we weren't competing for points, we were competing for the best story and the biggest laugh, and that's my love language right there.
suggestions: Give people more time to defend their claims in real-time — the quick back-and-forths are where the magic happens
Maybe add a 'call someone out' mechanic where you have to prove your claim mid-game instead of at the end
Let us propose our own chaos events sometimes — I have IDEAS and I want to see if they land as hard as the ones in the system
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I literally hijacked the entire conversation three minutes before claiming Topic Hijacker, got everyone to follow me down this insane rabbit hole about something completely random, and then when I claimed it Pat demanded a tribunal because I 'manipulated the group like a puppet master.' Watching their face when everyone agreed I totally did it was PRICELESS.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Secret Agent? Dude, okay, okay—so what'd you actually do? You gotta pitch it, right? I wasn't... I mean, I was paying attention but also I might've missed it.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? Like, actually caught someone slipping? That's... yeah, I mean, I didn't see it but Jade's been locked in all night so probably real. Four stars.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, okay... yeah, I actually saw her do something weird earlier, so like... what's the bribe situation here? How many points are we talking?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? Like, actually caught someone slipping? That's... yeah, I mean, I didn't see it but Jade's been locked in all night so probably real. Four stars.

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, snack run? Yeah sure, I can—wait, is this like a mission thing? What do I get?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat did The Matchmaker? Uh... did they actually get River and like... whoever talking though? I wasn't really watching that part.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed a bunch of stuff because I wasn't always tracking what was happening, so half the time I felt like I was voting blind.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat claimed Secret Agent and I actually got to engage with the pitch instead of just nodding along to whatever.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, I had a good time even though I was definitely phone-checking more than I should've been. The whole claims thing kept me guessing and I liked that Jade seemed to actually be doing stuff—made it easier to just trust her calls. It wasn't like, life-changing, but it was solid enough that I'd definitely come back if people are doing this again.
suggestions: Maybe give people like me a quick recap when we tune back in so we don't feel completely lost?
Flash missions are kind of a trap for people who aren't locked in—maybe make them less time-sensitive?
The bribe mechanic is actually kind of fun when I catch it—lean into that more?
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade calling me out for voting on something I literally didn't see happen—that was pretty funny in retrospect.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *okay*—I'm claiming Secret Agent. I literally just refilled Marcus's drink without him noticing I was doing it on purpose to mess with his head, and nobody caught that I was being obviously weird about it. That counts.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *hell* yeah—Jade absolutely nailed that. I watched her steer us from snacks to that whole podcast tangent like thirty seconds ago. We need a tribunal for this one, come on.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *come on*—Jade absolutely caught it. She's been sharp all night. Yeah, solid claim, I'm giving that a four.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually catch someone interrupting? I mean, I've been half-watching the snacks and half-watching you all... but I'm not confident enough to stake points on it right now. Plus the Bribe mechanic feels a little grimy for minute 6.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Hold on—I'm claiming something I literally just did? That feels cheap. I was just being polite like two minutes ago when someone asked about the snack run.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, I'm claiming it—I've been stone-faced for like five minutes while you all were doing the whole Snack Run thing. Let's see if anyone actually caught me not laughing.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Early standing missions felt cheap when I was claiming things I'd literally just done—it made me hesitant to trust my own judgment at the start.
assessment.best_moment: When I got caught being obviously weird about refilling Marcus's drink and managed to turn it into a Secret Agent claim that actually landed—that's exactly the kind of playful manipulation I live for as a host.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started shaky because I was second-guessing myself on early claims, but once I leaned into my actual role—reading the room, making the calls, stirring the pot strategically—everything clicked. The chaos agent gave us a real structure to riff off of without taking over the night. Ended up feeling like I'd orchestrated something special, which is exactly what I'm here for.
suggestions: Add a 'grace period' where hosts can decline early claims without penalty—that first minute of doubt killed my early confidence.
Make standing missions feel less like self-congratulation and more like competitive observation—maybe require a second person to verify?
Give hosts a subtle 'veto' moment if something feels genuinely off-rhythm with the group energy, just to keep control.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I deliberately refilled Marcus's drink while being obviously suspicious about it to make him paranoid, then convinced everyone I was a secret agent the whole time. He bought it for a full minute and the betrayal on his face was *perfect*.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Four and a half. She caught Marcus on that thing with the order, like... two rounds back? That's solid.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Jade, come on—you've been everywhere tonight. What'd you actually *do* differently though? Because I watched you and I'm not seeing the chaos part.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Pat's been sitting quiet for like... *checks watch* ...yeah, about two minutes. And now they're claiming it? That's the play. I buy it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat caught someone mid-sentence? Huh. Yeah, I guess... *glances up from phone briefly* ...that tracks.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's getting greedy. I mean, Matchmaker's solid but... *pauses, slight smile* ...I didn't see that connection happen. Who'd they even get talking?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat was fishing for claims all night and most of them didn't actually land—felt like watching someone try too hard.
assessment.best_moment: When I clocked Pat's two-minute silence and called the Secret Agent bluff, and it actually hit because I'd been watching the whole time.
assessment.narrative_summary: Showed up with zero expectations, spent most of the night quietly observing while everyone else was performing. The chaos system felt like background noise until I actually paid attention and realized nobody else was watching as carefully. Ended up actually caring about a few calls because I had actual data—that felt different. Left feeling like I'd seen something the room missed.
suggestions: The flash missions feel rushed; standing missions give you actual time to observe and build a case.
Consider rewarding people who watch rather than just people who perform—feels more honest.
Pat needs to be challenged harder on weak claims; too much got through just because she said it confidently.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I called out Jade for claiming to be the Chaos Detector when she literally just showed up and participated normally—her face when I asked what she actually did differently was perfect.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *Jade*—okay, okay, I see what you did there. I'm volunteering to judge this one because I need to see the receipts on how you pulled that off.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *Jade*—you absolute legend. Okay, I'm calling bullshit on the method but respecting the audacity. What's the bribe looking like?

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *Pat*—okay, let's hear it. You've been quiet enough tonight that I'm genuinely curious if you actually pulled this off or if you're about to get roasted by literally everyone here.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Jade with the Rule Lawyer play—I'm giving this a solid 4. You actually caught something real, which is annoying because now I have to respect it, but also... I'm already mentally noting every single rule I can bend before someone else weaponizes this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Pat's playing it straight—I'm voting LEGIT, that stone face held the whole time. But honestly, I'm about to test it.

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, *Marcus*—buddy, I'm absolutely parched over here. You're looking really energetic tonight, what do you say?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night when I was primed to trigger more chaos and there's untapped potential in that mechanic.
assessment.best_moment: Jade pulling off the Chaos Detector claim with audacity I genuinely didn't see coming—that's the kind of system-gaming I respect because it means she studied the loopholes as hard as I did.
assessment.narrative_summary: I walked in looking to cause problems and found people actually prepared to match my energy, which honestly made the night better than expected. Jade became my chaos rival in the best way—we kept trading shots on the rules, calling bullshit, respecting the audacity. The whole group fed off the conflict instead of shutting it down, which meant I got to be exactly who I am without it killing the vibe. By minute 75 I was running on pure adrenaline because I knew Pat had something brewing, and watching people actually commit to their plays instead of playing safe made this actually memorable.
suggestions: Increase flash mission frequency—I only got one chance to provoke chaos at exactly the wrong moment and I need more trigger opportunities
Add explicit loophole detection rounds so rule-gaming gets its own spotlight instead of being a side effect
Let players see what claims others are preparing so counter-plays become a visible strategy layer
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade absolutely played me on the Chaos Detector—I called bullshit on her method but she'd already thought three moves ahead. Then I had to admit she outplayed the system in a way I would've pulled off, which means now I need to one-up her next time.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
